input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. Let me provide guidance based on my knowledge.

In .NET 9 and earlier, MAUI used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` on Android to resize the window when the keyboard appeared. However, `Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ and was removed/stopped working in .NET 10.


The recommended approach in .NET 10 is to use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Pan` instead, or handle keyboard insets manually using the Android `WindowInsets` API. The shift happened because Android deprecated the resize approach, so MAUI moved toward edge-to-edge display with proper inset handling for keyboard visibility.

For a chat screen where the input bar needs to move up with the keyboard, I can use `Pan` mode as the simpler option, though it may not work perfectly for all layouts. The more robust solution involves reacting to keyboard visibility through the platform APIs.

Setting up the Pan mode... The key insight here is that .NET 10 MAUI's edge-to-edge rendering by default breaks the traditional `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` approach on Android, so I need to listen to `WindowInsets` changes and respond to keyboard visibility instead.
